mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting higher corporate revenues and generating positive quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or quarterly returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening periods, producing negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low policy rate creates negative real interest rates and signals delayed central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation/low-rate periods, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth falling below 1.0 percent annualized signals severe demand contraction and recession risk, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 70% of sub-1% GDP growth periods, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and generating negative monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.
